channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UK Youth Fund 2026: £10 Million Grant Supporting Small Youth Organisations Across the United Kingdom
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The UK Youth, in partnership with the Pears Foundation, has launched the UK Youth Fund, a transformative £10 million funding initiative aimed at strengthening small youth organisations across the United Kingdom. This ambitious programme is designed to provide multi-year unrestricted funding combined with targeted capacity-building support, helping grassroots organisations build resilience, sustainability, and long-term impact […]

The post UK Youth Fund 2026: £10 Million Grant Supporting Small Youth Organisations Across the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UK Youth, in partnership with the Pears Foundation, has launched the UK Youth Fund, a transformative £10 million funding initiative aimed at strengthening small youth organisations across the United Kingdom.

This ambitious programme is designed to provide multi-year unrestricted funding combined with targeted capacity-building support, helping grassroots organisations build resilience, sustainability, and long-term impact for young people.

 What is the UK Youth Fund?

The UK Youth Fund is a strategic funding initiative focused on empowering small organisations that deliver vital services to young people. Unlike traditional grants, this fund offers flexible, unrestricted funding, allowing organisations to allocate resources where they are most needed.

This approach recognizes that grassroots organisations are best positioned to understand their own challenges and opportunities, enabling them to:

  • Strengthen core operations
  • Invest in innovation
  • Improve long-term sustainability
  • Expand their impact on young people

The fund builds on a successful partnership between UK Youth and Pears Foundation, which has already delivered impactful programmes such as the COVID Relief Fund (£2.2 million) and the Cost-of-Living Fund (£7 million).

 Objectives of the Fund

The UK Youth Fund is structured around two key outcomes:

1. Improved Financial Sustainability

Through multi-year unrestricted funding, organisations can:

  • Plan strategically for long-term growth
  • Stabilize operations
  • Allocate resources flexibly

2. Increased Capability and Organisational Resilience

Through capacity-building support, organisations can:

  • Strengthen internal systems
  • Improve leadership and governance
  • Enhance service delivery for young people

 Funding Details

Eligible organisations can apply for multi-year unrestricted funding of up to:

10% of their annual turnover

This proportional funding model ensures:

  • Fair distribution of resources
  • Sustainability aligned with organisational size
  • Long-term financial resilience

Organisations are encouraged to use the funding in ways that maximize impact, whether through:

  • Operational strengthening
  • Programme development
  • Innovation initiatives

 Who Can Apply?

The fund is specifically designed for small youth organisations operating in the UK.

Eligibility criteria include:

  • Annual turnover of less than £500,000
  • Delivery of services to young people
  • Operations within eligible communities across:
    • England
    • Scotland
    • Wales
    • Northern Ireland

Importantly, eligibility is based on the communities served, not the organisation’s headquarters.

Even organisations supporting just one young person from eligible areas can apply, making the fund highly inclusive and accessible.

 Application Process

The UK Youth Fund uses a two-stage application process designed to be accessible and efficient:

Stage 1: Expression of Interest (EOI)

  • Short eligibility check and multiple-choice questions
  • Confirms whether organisations meet criteria

Stage 2: Full Application

  • Invited applicants submit detailed proposals
  • Focus on impact, sustainability, and organisational needs

This structure reduces unnecessary workload and ensures only eligible organisations proceed.

 Key Dates

  • Expression of Interest opens: April 14, 2026 (2:00 PM)
  • EOI Deadline: May 6, 2026 (11:59 PM)
  • Application Invitation: May 11, 2026
  • Final Application Deadline: June 3, 2026 (11:59 PM)

Applications must be submitted via the official online grant system.

 Information Webinar

Applicants can attend an online information session on April 21, 2026 (2:00 PM) to:

  • Learn more about the fund
  • Understand eligibility criteria
  • Ask questions directly to the grants team

 Why This Fund Matters

The UK Youth Fund arrives at a critical time when youth organisations face increasing demand and financial pressure. By offering flexible, long-term funding, the programme enables organisations to:

  • Provide stable and safe spaces for young people
  • Address social and economic challenges
  • Deliver impactful youth services
  • Build sustainable futures for their communities

This initiative represents a major investment in the future of youth work across the UK, ensuring that grassroots organisations can continue transforming young lives.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post UK Youth Fund 2026: £10 Million Grant Supporting Small Youth Organisations Across the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026: Call for Mentors to Shape Europe’s Next Generation of Women Innovators
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The European Innovation Council (EIC) has officially launched its 2026 Call for Mentors under the highly impactful EIC Women Leadership Programme, inviting experienced professionals across Europe’s innovation ecosystem to contribute to the development of high-potential women researchers and entrepreneurs. Running from May to November 2026, this prestigious initiative offers a unique opportunity for leaders to […]

The post EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026: Call for Mentors to Shape Europe’s Next Generation of Women Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Innovation Council (EIC) has officially launched its 2026 Call for Mentors under the highly impactful EIC Women Leadership Programme, inviting experienced professionals across Europe’s innovation ecosystem to contribute to the development of high-potential women researchers and entrepreneurs.

Running from May to November 2026, this prestigious initiative offers a unique opportunity for leaders to actively shape the future of European innovation while expanding their own influence, visibility, and professional network.

 What is the EIC Women Leadership Programme?

The EIC Women Leadership Programme is a flagship initiative by the European Innovation Council aimed at empowering women innovators by strengthening their leadership skills, expanding their networks, and supporting their entrepreneurial growth.

Since its launch in 2021, the programme has successfully supported over 300 women researchers and entrepreneurs, creating a dynamic and inclusive ecosystem where innovation thrives through diversity and collaboration.

A central pillar of the programme is its structured mentoring scheme, which connects:

  • Experienced leaders (mentors)
  • High-potential women innovators (mentees)

This exchange fosters meaningful collaboration, knowledge transfer, and long-term professional relationships.

 Purpose of the Mentor Call

The 2026 call seeks highly experienced professionals willing to contribute their expertise on a pro bono basis while engaging in a high-level European innovation network.

Mentors play a critical role in:

  • Guiding mentees through leadership challenges
  • Supporting business and venture growth
  • Enhancing strategic thinking and decision-making
  • Expanding mentees’ professional networks

Through this engagement, mentors directly contribute to building a stronger, more inclusive European innovation landscape.

 What’s in It for Mentors?

Participating as a mentor offers both professional recognition and strategic opportunities, including:

  • Official mentor recognition and certification
  • Visibility across EIC communication channels (including mentor showcases)
  • Access to an exclusive European network of investors, founders, and innovation leaders
  • Invitations to speaking engagements and thought leadership opportunities
  • Exposure to cutting-edge technologies and breakthrough innovations
  • Participation in prestigious events such as the EIC Summit
  • Opportunity to shape the future of women’s leadership in innovation

This is not just a mentoring role—it is a strategic leadership position within Europe’s innovation ecosystem.

 The Role of a Mentor

Mentors are matched with participants based on their expertise and the mentees’ development needs, ensuring a high-impact and personalized mentoring relationship.

Key areas of focus include:

  • Leadership development and confident decision-making
  • Strategic thinking and business growth
  • Career advancement and leadership positioning
  • Network expansion and visibility

Mentors are expected to share insights, provide guidance, and open doors to opportunities that can accelerate the mentees’ professional journeys.

 Programme Timeline

The mentoring journey is structured to ensure meaningful engagement while respecting mentors’ time:

  • 13 May 2026 – Online onboarding workshop
  • 30 June 2026 – Mentor–mentee matching confirmed
  • July – November 2026 – Mentoring sessions and networking activities
  • 27 November 2026 – Final event, Demo Day, and pitching session (online)

 Time Commitment

The programme is designed to be flexible and manageable:

  • 5–6 online mentoring sessions
  • Each session lasts approximately 1 hour
  • Scheduling is flexible based on mentor availability

 Who Should Apply?

The programme is open to experienced professionals from across Europe’s innovation ecosystem, including:

  • Established entrepreneurs and founders
  • Investors and venture capital professionals
  • C-level executives and senior managers
  • Innovation experts and ecosystem leaders

Ideal candidates demonstrate:

  • Strong leadership experience
  • Deep understanding of innovation and entrepreneurship
  • Commitment to supporting the next generation of women leaders

 Application Process

Applications must be submitted through the official EIC application platform, where candidates can provide details about their expertise, experience, and motivation to join the programme.

After submission:

  • Applications undergo a selection process
  • Selected mentors are invited to onboarding
  • Matching with mentees is conducted based on expertise and needs

 Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

April 30, 2026 

Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is strongly recommended.

 Why This Opportunity Matters

The EIC Women Leadership Programme represents a powerful step toward:

  • Promoting gender equality in innovation
  • Supporting women in leadership and entrepreneurship
  • Building inclusive and diverse innovation ecosystems

By becoming a mentor, you are not only giving back—you are actively shaping the future of European innovation leadership.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE

Discover more opportunities here 

The post EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026: Call for Mentors to Shape Europe’s Next Generation of Women Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Europe for Early-Career Academics from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The prestigious Coimbra Group has officially launched its 2026 Scholarship Programme, offering a unique and highly competitive opportunity for early-career professors and researchers from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood to advance their academic careers through international collaboration. This initiative stands as one of Europe’s most recognized academic mobility programs, designed to strengthen global […]

The post Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Europe for Early-Career Academics from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The prestigious Coimbra Group has officially launched its 2026 Scholarship Programme, offering a unique and highly competitive opportunity for early-career professors and researchers from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood to advance their academic careers through international collaboration.

This initiative stands as one of Europe’s most recognized academic mobility programs, designed to strengthen global research partnerships and promote academic excellence across continents.

 What is the Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme?

The Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme is an annual initiative led by a network of leading European universities. These institutions are known for their long-standing academic traditions, multidisciplinary excellence, and commitment to fostering international cooperation.

For 2026, the programme offers 74 fully supported scholarships, enabling selected candidates to undertake short-term research visits at one of 21 top European universities that are members of the Coimbra Group.

These scholarships are specifically designed for:

  • Early-career academics
  • University staff engaged in teaching and research
  • Scholars seeking international exposure and collaboration

 Objectives of the Programme

The Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme goes beyond financial support—it is a strategic academic initiative with multiple objectives:

  • Internationalization of Research: Scholars can expand the global reach of their work by collaborating with European institutions.
  • Academic Networking: Participants build lasting partnerships with leading researchers and institutions.
  • Knowledge Exchange: The programme fosters cross-cultural academic dialogue and innovation.
  • Career Advancement: Exposure to European research environments strengthens academic profiles and future opportunities.

 Host Institutions

Participants will conduct their research at one of the Coimbra Group’s 21 member universities, located across Europe. These institutions are recognized for:

  • High academic standards
  • Cutting-edge research facilities
  • Strong international collaboration networks

Each university offers a unique research environment, allowing scholars to align their projects with institutional expertise.

 What Does the Scholarship Cover?

While specific funding details may vary depending on the host university, the scholarship typically includes:

  • Financial support for the duration of the research stay
  • Access to university research facilities and resources
  • Opportunities to collaborate with leading European academics
  • Academic mentorship and supervision

The programme is designed to reduce financial barriers and allow scholars to fully focus on their research and collaboration goals.

 Application Process

Applications must be submitted online via the official Coimbra Group website, where candidates can:

  • Select their preferred host university
  • Review eligibility requirements
  • Access detailed application guidelines
  • Upload required documents

Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review the specific requirements of each participating university before applying.

 Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

May 10th, 2026 (midnight CEST)

Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants should plan ahead and ensure all documents are completed and uploaded on time.

 Why You Should Apply

This programme represents a rare opportunity to:

  • Conduct research in Europe without long-term relocation
  • Gain international academic exposure
  • Strengthen your research impact and visibility
  • Join a global network of scholars and institutions

For early-career researchers aiming to elevate their academic journey, the Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026 is a powerful stepping stone toward global recognition and collaboration.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Coimbra Group Scholarship Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunities in Europe for Early-Career Academics from Africa, Latin America, and the European Neighbourhood appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New Opportunities at the Danish Institute for Human Rights – International Activities
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are seeking a high-impact international career in European affairs, human rights, and public engagement, this EU Programme Officer role presents a compelling opportunity. Hosted by the Danish Institute for Human Rights, this position focuses on the Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values (CERV) Programme—one of the European Union’s flagship funding instruments supporting democracy, equality, […]

The post New Opportunities at the Danish Institute for Human Rights – International Activities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are seeking a high-impact international career in European affairs, human rights, and public engagement, this EU Programme Officer role presents a compelling opportunity. Hosted by the Danish Institute for Human Rights, this position focuses on the Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values (CERV) Programme—one of the European Union’s flagship funding instruments supporting democracy, equality, and civic participation across Europe.

This role is ideal for professionals with strong communication skills, a background in political science or humanities, and a passion for EU programmes, stakeholder engagement, and fundraising. Based in Copenhagen, Denmark, the job offers both national and international exposure, making it a strategic career move for those aiming to work at the intersection of policy, rights, and EU funding mechanisms.

1. EU Engagement & CERV  Programme Officer

The EU Engagement & CERV Programme Officer will play a central role in implementing Denmark’s National Contact Point mandate for the EU CERV Programme. The position combines communication, outreach, policy interpretation, and fundraising support within an internationally respected human rights institution.

You will act as a bridge between EU funding structures and Danish stakeholders, helping organizations understand, access, and benefit from EU opportunities related to equality, rights, and civic engagement.

About the CERV Programme

The Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values (CERV) Programme (2021–2027) is an EU initiative designed to:

  • Promote citizens’ engagement and participation in democratic life
  • Advance equality and combat discrimination
  • Protect and promote fundamental rights
  • Strengthen civil society across EU member states

As part of the Danish National Contact Point, you will help organizations navigate this programme, ensuring they can successfully apply for and implement EU-funded projects.

About the Danish Institute for Human Rights

The Danish Institute for Human Rights is Denmark’s national human rights institution, operating independently under a legal mandate. It plays a critical global role in promoting and implementing human rights standards across governments, NGOs, and private sector actors.

The Institute is especially recognized for:

  • Driving sustainable, long-term human rights solutions
  • Collaborating across sectors (public, private, and civil society)
  • Working at the intersection of business, law, and international policy

Key Responsibilities

1. Communication & Outreach Strategy

  • Lead communication efforts for the CERV programme across Denmark
  • Manage online platforms, publications, and awareness campaigns
  • Organize and participate in national events, including Folkemødet

2. EU Programme Advisory

  • Interpret EU funding priorities and technical requirements
  • Guide Danish stakeholders on funding opportunities and compliance
  • Translate complex EU frameworks into accessible information

3. Thematic Expertise

  • Provide insights on:
    • Equality and non-discrimination
    • Civic participation
    • Fundamental rights and EU values

4. Public Engagement

  • Deliver presentations, seminars, and workshops
  • Represent the programme at national and EU-level events

5. Fundraising & Strategic Development

  • Support donor mapping and screening
  • Develop donor profiles and external relations data
  • Contribute to institutional fundraising strategies

6. Project Implementation

  • Assist in planning and executing EU-funded projects
  • Ensure compliance with EU financial and operational requirements

Required Qualifications

Academic Requirements

  • Master’s degree in:
    • Political Science
    • Humanities
    • Or a related field

Professional Experience

  • Background in:
    • Communication
    • Editorial work
    • Public outreach

Core Skills

  • Excellent written and verbal communication in:
    • English
    • Danish
  • Strong digital and communication tools proficiency
  • High level of organization and ability to work independently

Knowledge & Interests

  • Demonstrated interest in:
    • European Union affairs
    • Human rights and equality
    • Civic participation and democracy
  • Willingness to learn EU funding systems and compliance frameworks

Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply?

Category of Applicants

This opportunity is best suited for:

  • Early to mid-career professionals
  • EU affairs specialists
  • Human rights practitioners
  • Communication and outreach experts
  • Policy and governance professionals

Geographic Eligibility

  • Primarily open to candidates eligible to work in Denmark
  • Strong requirement for Danish language proficiency suggests preference for:
    • Danish nationals
    • EU/EEA candidates with Danish fluency
  • International applicants may be considered if they meet language and work authorization requirements

Job Location and Terms

  • Location: Christianshavn, Copenhagen, Denmark
  • Employment Type: Full-time (37 hours per week)
  • Start Date: 1 June 2026 or as soon as possible
  • Salary: Based on Danish Ministry of Finance agreements, with potential for negotiation

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

  • Direct involvement with EU funding programmes
  • Strong career pathway into international policy and human rights work
  • Opportunity to influence civic participation and equality initiatives
  • Exposure to high-level stakeholders across government, NGOs, and EU institutions
  • Work within a globally respected human rights institution

Application Process

Required Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Cover letter (in English)
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Academic diplomas
  • Academic transcripts/grades

Submission Details

  • Applications must be submitted via the official application portal
  • Deadline: 22 April 2026 at 12:00 (GMT+1)

Interview Timeline

  • Interviews are expected to take place during the last week of April 2026

Apply here

Deadline

22nd April 2026

Equal Opportunity Statement

The Danish Institute for Human Rights is committed to diversity and inclusion. All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply regardless of background. This EU Programme Officer role is more than just a job—it is a gateway into the world of European funding, international development, and human rights advocacy. With its blend of communication, policy, and strategic engagement, it offers a unique platform to make meaningful contributions at both national and European levels.

2. Internship in Human Rights – Business & Tech

If you are passionate about human rights, business ethics, and the intersection of technology and global development, the Internship in Human Rights, Business and Tech – Fall 2026 at the Danish Institute for Human Rights offers an exceptional opportunity to gain hands-on international experience. This in-depth guide provides everything you need to know—from eligibility and responsibilities to application strategy and deadlines.

Internship Focus: Human Rights, Business, and Technology

This internship is based within the Human Rights, Business and Tech Department, where selected interns will actively contribute to integrating human rights standards into global business practices.

A key component of the role is exposure to the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights, the leading international framework guiding corporate responsibility in human rights.

What You Will Do (Roles and Responsibilities)

Interns will be fully integrated into project teams and supervised throughout the internship. Your work will be divided into two major areas:

1. Research and Analytical Work (70–80%)

You will engage in high-level research across multiple domains, including:

  • Human rights issues in sectors such as:
    • Renewable energy
    • Technology
    • Finance
  • Gender and business-related human rights concerns
  • Country-specific human rights challenges
  • Corporate human rights due diligence practices
  • Legal and policy frameworks:
    • National Action Plans on Business and Human Rights
    • International regulatory standards

2. Administrative and Operational Support (20–30%)

You will also support organizational functions such as:

  • Coordinating meetings and international events
  • Archiving documents and managing communications
  • Proofreading and referencing research outputs
  • Drafting reports and taking meeting minutes
  • Assisting in translation work where applicable

Skills and Competencies You Will Gain

This internship is designed to build both academic and professional capabilities:

  • Advanced research and policy analysis skills
  • Practical application of human rights frameworks
  • Experience in international, multicultural work environments
  • Communication and reporting expertise
  • Time management and prioritization in professional settings

Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply?

Academic Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold a Bachelor’s degree (BA) in:
    • Human Rights
    • Law
    • Political Science
    • Development Studies
    • Or a related field
  • Be currently enrolled in a Master’s degree program at the time of the internship
  • Be able to transfer the internship into academic credits

Professional and Technical Skills

Ideal candidates should have:

  • Strong academic or practical interest in human rights or sustainable development
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Fluency in English (mandatory)

Additional language advantages:

  • Spanish
  • French
  • Danish

Additional Preferred Experience

  • Exposure to business environments or corporate responsibility
  • Experience with NGOs, development organizations, or human rights initiatives
  • Participation in extracurricular or volunteer activities related to social justice

Eligible Applicants by Region

This opportunity is open globally, meaning applicants can come from:

  • Africa
  • Europe
  • Asia
  • North America
  • South America
  • Oceania

However, international applicants must:

  • Be able to obtain a Danish work permit
  • Secure external funding or scholarships to cover living costs (since the internship is unpaid)

Important Note on Funding

This internship is unpaid, which is standard in Denmark where domestic students often receive government stipends.

International applicants are strongly encouraged to:

  • Apply for scholarships or grants
  • Seek university funding support
  • Explore external fellowship opportunities

Work Environment and Benefits

While unpaid, the internship offers significant non-financial benefits:

  • Work in a globally respected human rights institution
  • Gain exposure to international policy and corporate responsibility
  • Build a strong professional network
  • Collaborate with experts across sectors and regions
  • Develop career-relevant, real-world experience

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit:

  • A CV (Curriculum Vitae)
  • A Cover Letter, clearly outlining:
    • Your motivation
    • Relevant experience
    • Availability (start and end dates)
    • Any academic obligations during the internship

Applications must be submitted via the official application portal on the Institute’s website.

Apply here

Selection Process

  • Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews
  • Interviews will take place within 14 days after the deadline
  • Interviews may be conducted:
    • In person
    • Via Microsoft Teams

Deadline

23rd April 2026

This internship is ideal for students seeking a serious, career-defining experience in the field of business and human rights. It is especially valuable for those aiming to work in:

  • International organizations
  • Policy and governance
  • Corporate social responsibility
  • Human rights advocacy

Despite being unpaid, the depth of exposure, training, and global relevance makes this one of the most impactful internship opportunities in Europe for 2026.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post New Opportunities at the Danish Institute for Human Rights – International Activities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CIVICUS Global Job Opportunities 2026: High-Paying International NGO Roles
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are looking for a prestigious international career in human rights, civil society, and global development, opportunities at CIVICUS offer one of the most impactful pathways. This detailed guide explores everything you need to know about CIVICUS job opportunities, including eligibility criteria, qualifications, global accessibility, deadlines, and how to successfully apply. Overview of CIVICUS […]

The post CIVICUS Global Job Opportunities 2026: High-Paying International NGO Roles appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are looking for a prestigious international career in human rights, civil society, and global development, opportunities at CIVICUS offer one of the most impactful pathways. This detailed guide explores everything you need to know about CIVICUS job opportunities, including eligibility criteria, qualifications, global accessibility, deadlines, and how to successfully apply.

Overview of CIVICUS and Why These Opportunities Matter

CIVICUS is a globally recognized international non-profit organization headquartered in Johannesburg, South Africa. Founded in 1993, it works to strengthen citizen action and civil society worldwide, operating in more than 180 countries with over 15,000 members.

The organization focuses on:

  • Promoting human rights and civic freedoms
  • Strengthening civil society organizations
  • Supporting activism and citizen participation
  • Amplifying voices from underrepresented regions, especially the Global South

Because of its global reach and mission, working with CIVICUS provides exposure to international policy, advocacy, and development work at the highest level.

CIVICUS offers multiple employment categories, including:

  • Full-time jobs
  • Consultancy roles
  • Internships
  • Project-based contracts

These are professional career opportunities within a global non-profit environment.

Current Featured Opportunity Examples

1. Communications Consultants

2. Media Consultants

3. Web Developers

4. Designers & Ilustrators

5. Translators & Editors

6. Interpreters

7. Videographers & Film makers

8. Creative Meeting Facilitators

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants)

CIVICUS opportunities are open to a wide range of professionals, including:

1. Early-Career Applicants

  • Recent graduates
  • Entry-level professionals
  • Interns interested in global development

2. Mid-Level Professionals

  • NGO practitioners
  • Communications specialists
  • Project managers
  • Policy analysts

3. Senior Experts

  • Advocacy leaders
  • Human rights specialists
  • Senior consultants and strategists

4. Students and Interns

  • Undergraduate and postgraduate students
  • Individuals seeking hands-on NGO experience

Eligible Countries and Regions

CIVICUS is a global organization, and its opportunities are generally open to applicants from:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Europe
  • North America
  • South America
  • Oceania

However, there are important conditions:

  • Many roles are location-specific (e.g., Johannesburg, Geneva, New York)
  • Applicants may need legal authorization to work in the country of employment
  • In some cases, CIVICUS may assist with work permits depending on the role and candidate profile

Key Qualifications and Requirements

While requirements vary by role, CIVICUS typically looks for the following:

Educational Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree (minimum requirement for most roles)
  • Master’s degree preferred for senior roles
  • Relevant fields include:
    • International relations
    • Political science
    • Development studies
    • Law
    • Human rights
    • Media and communications

      Professional Experience

      Entry-Level / Internships:

        • Basic understanding of NGO or development work
        • Strong communication
        • Familiarity with tools like MS Office and collaboration platforms

      Mid-Level Roles:

          • 3–6 years of relevant experience
          • Project coordination or communications expertise
          • Experience in civil society or advocacy

      Senior Roles:

        • 6+ years of experience
        • Leadership or management experience
        • Deep understanding of global governance and human rights systems

Core Skills Required

Across most roles, CIVICUS emphasizes:

  • Excellent written and spoken English
  • Strong communication and storytelling skills
  • Digital literacy (MS Office, collaboration tools)
  • Ability to work in multicultural, remote teams
  • Project management and organizational skills
  • Knowledge of global civil society trends
  • Commitment to human rights and civic engagement

Additional Preferred Skills

  • Knowledge of UN systems and international policy
  • Multilingual ability (French, Spanish, etc.)
  • Experience with advocacy campaigns
  • Media and communications expertise

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

To apply for a CIVICUS opportunity:

Step 1: Visit the Official Careers Page

Go to the CIVICUS “Work With Us” section to view open vacancies.

Step 2: Select a Specific Vacancy

CIVICUS does not accept speculative applications. You must apply for a listed position.

Step 3: Prepare Required Documents

You will need:

  • A detailed CV
  • A one-page cover letter explaining:
    • Your motivation
    • Relevant experience
    • Fit for the role

Step 4: Submit Your Application

Follow instructions in the job listing (usually via email or application portal).

Step 5: Wait for Feedback

  • Expected response time: within 3 weeks after deadline
  • If no response is received, consider the application unsuccessful

Apply here

Why You Should Apply to CIVICUS

Working with CIVICUS offers:

  • Global exposure in international development
  • Opportunities to influence human rights and policy
  • Collaboration with professionals across 180+ countries
  • Remote and flexible work options
  • Career growth in the NGO and international sector

Deadline

24th April 2026

CIVICUS job opportunities are among the most competitive and impactful roles in the global civil society ecosystem. Whether you are an entry-level candidate or a seasoned professional, these positions provide a unique platform to contribute to meaningful global change.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post CIVICUS Global Job Opportunities 2026: High-Paying International NGO Roles appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ITR Fellowship 4.0 Application 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Fellowship Empowering Young Africans with Leadership, Skills and Internship Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The ITR Fellowship 4.0 is a transformative virtual program designed to empower young Africans with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities required to become impactful leaders and change-makers in their communities. This fellowship stands out as a comprehensive personal and professional development initiative aimed at individuals who are at the early stages of their journey and […]

The post ITR Fellowship 4.0 Application 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Fellowship Empowering Young Africans with Leadership, Skills and Internship Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ITR Fellowship 4.0 is a transformative virtual program designed to empower young Africans with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities required to become impactful leaders and change-makers in their communities. This fellowship stands out as a comprehensive personal and professional development initiative aimed at individuals who are at the early stages of their journey and seeking clarity, direction, and capacity-building.

With a strong focus on leadership development, social innovation, and real-world impact, the program is structured to guide participants from a place of uncertainty to one of purpose, equipping them with practical tools to create meaningful change.

Category of Opportunity

Fellowship (with Internship Opportunity)

This program falls under the fellowship category, with an added advantage of a post-program internship placement for successful participants.

Overview of the ITR Fellowship 4.0

The ITR Fellowship is an 8-week virtual experience tailored specifically for young Africans who are eager to grow personally and contribute to societal development. The fourth cohort introduces an exciting expansion — an internship opportunity for fellows who meet the program’s selection and participation criteria.

The fellowship is designed to:

  • Provide clarity and direction for young individuals
  • Build leadership capacity and confidence
  • Equip participants with practical skills for social impact
  • Foster innovation and community engagement
  • Create pathways for mentorship and career development

Fellowship Benefits

Participants selected for the ITR Fellowship 4.0 will gain access to a wide range of benefits, including:

1. Community Impact Project

Fellows will have the opportunity to design and execute a project that addresses a real issue within their community. This hands-on experience allows participants to apply learned concepts and create measurable impact.

2. Post-Fellowship Mentorship

Graduates of the program will receive continued guidance from industry experts. This mentorship helps fellows refine their goals, improve their strategies, and navigate their career or impact journey effectively.

3. Internship Placement Opportunity

One of the most valuable additions in this cohort is the internship experience. Fellows who meet the program’s requirements will be matched with organizations where they can:

  • Gain practical work experience
  • Develop professional skills
  • Build a strong portfolio
  • Enhance employability

Who Should Apply?

This fellowship is specifically targeted at:

  • Young Africans who feel under-resourced in personal development
  • Individuals who lack access to mentorship and skill-building opportunities
  • Aspiring change-makers who want to create impact but do not know where to start
  • Youth from humble backgrounds or underrepresented communities

If you are motivated, eager to learn, and committed to making a difference, this fellowship is designed for you.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the ITR Fellowship 4.0, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Must be an African (from any country within the continent)
  • Must be between 18 and 30 years old
  • Must have proficiency in speaking and understanding English
  • Must have little or no prior experience in community development
  • Must be available and willing to commit to the full 8-week duration of the fellowship
  • Must maintain at least 85% attendance to qualify for graduation, mentorship, and internship opportunities
  • Must demonstrate strong motivation and willingness to contribute to social change and innovation

Program Structure and Expectations

The fellowship runs for a period of 8 weeks and is conducted virtually, making it accessible to participants across Africa. During the program, fellows will engage in:

  • Interactive training sessions
  • Personal development workshops
  • Leadership and communication training
  • Project design and execution
  • Networking opportunities with fellow participants

Attendance and active participation are critical, as they directly impact eligibility for post-fellowship benefits.

Application Instructions

Applicants must carefully follow the instructions below:

  • Complete all sections of the application form electronically
  • Submit all responses in English language
  • Provide honest and original answers

Important Notice

Applicants are explicitly advised not to use AI tools in completing their application. Any applicant found violating this rule will be disqualified.

Apply here

Testimonials from Previous Fellows

Past participants of the ITR Fellowship have shared powerful experiences that highlight the program’s impact:

  • Fellows have reported significant growth in confidence, leadership ability, and financial management skills
  • Many participants have successfully launched community projects and initiatives
  • The fellowship has helped individuals discover their purpose and take actionable steps toward achieving it
  • Networking opportunities have led to collaborations and career advancements

These testimonials reinforce the fellowship’s effectiveness in shaping future leaders and change-makers across Africa.

Why You Should Apply

The ITR Fellowship 4.0 is more than just a training program. It is a life-changing opportunity that offers:

  • Personal clarity and direction
  • Practical leadership experience
  • Access to mentorship and professional networks
  • Real-world project execution
  • A pathway to internship and career growth

For young Africans seeking a breakthrough in their personal and professional journey, this fellowship provides a solid foundation to build upon.

Deadline

30th April 2026

The ITR Fellowship 4.0 represents a unique opportunity for young Africans to step into leadership, develop essential skills, and create meaningful impact in their communities. With its structured training, mentorship, and internship pathway, the program is designed to produce well-rounded individuals ready to drive change across the continent.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post ITR Fellowship 4.0 Application 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Fellowship Empowering Young Africans with Leadership, Skills and Internship Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 Grant for Disability Justice Projects (Up to $150,000)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Disability Inclusion Fund (DIF) 2026 Collaborative Grants Program, offering up to $150,000 over two years to support disability-led organizations advancing justice, inclusion, and cross-movement collaboration. The deadline to apply is May 20, 2026. Key Dates Application Deadline: May 20, 2026 Time: 8:59 PM PT | 9:59 PM CT | […]

The post Apply Now: Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 Grant for Disability Justice Projects (Up to $150,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Disability Inclusion Fund (DIF) 2026 Collaborative Grants Program, offering up to $150,000 over two years to support disability-led organizations advancing justice, inclusion, and cross-movement collaboration. The deadline to apply is May 20, 2026.

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: May 20, 2026
  • Time: 8:59 PM PT | 9:59 PM CT | 11:59 PM ET
  • Information Webinar: Held April 15, 2026 (recording available)
  • Notification of Results: By November 20, 2026

About Borealis Philanthropy

Borealis Philanthropy is a philanthropic intermediary that supports social justice movements across the United States. Through innovative funding strategies, Borealis invests in leaders, grassroots organizations, and collaborative efforts that drive transformative change—especially in disability justice and inclusion.

About the Disability Inclusion Fund (DIF)

The Disability Inclusion Fund supports U.S.-based, disability-led organizations working toward a more equitable and accessible society. Backed by major donors including the Presidents’ Council on Disability Inclusion in Philanthropy, DIF prioritizes initiatives that center lived experiences and build collective power across movements.

Core Values

All funded projects align with DIF’s guiding principles:

  • Participation: Centering those most impacted
  • Intersectionality: Recognizing overlapping identities
  • Radical Inclusion: Removing barriers to access
  • Leadership of the Most Impacted: Prioritizing marginalized voices
  • Cross-Movement Solidarity: Building alliances across sectors
  • Sustainability: Strengthening long-term movement capacity

What the DIF Collaborative Grants Fund

This opportunity supports joint proposals from at least two partner organizations working together toward shared impact.

Eligible Project Areas Include:

  • Disability justice initiatives led by and for disabled communities
  • Cross-movement work (e.g., racial justice, climate, labor, immigration, LGBTQ+ rights)
  • Coalition building and collaborative organizing
  • Narrative change to challenge ableism in culture and policy
  • Arts, media, and storytelling amplifying disabled voices
  • Advocacy, grassroots organizing, and policy change efforts

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must meet the following:

  • Partnership Requirement: At least two collaborating organizations
  • Leadership: At least one organization must be disability-led (50%+ leadership with disabilities)
  • Budget Cap: Each organization must have an annual budget under $3 million
  • Location: Must be U.S.-based or U.S. territory-based (or fiscally sponsored)
  • Project Focus: Must align with disability justice and collaborative impact

Additional Notes:

  • Partnerships may include universities, individuals, hospitals, or LLCs
  • However, the lead applicant must have a disability-centered mission
  • Projects primarily focused on research or direct service delivery are not eligible

Funding Details

  • Grant Size: Up to $75,000 per year
  • Duration: 2 years
  • Total Funding: Up to $150,000
  • Limit: Cannot exceed 25% of your organization’s total budget

Application Process

 How to Apply

Applicants must complete the online eligibility quiz and application form via Borealis Philanthropy’s portal.

Important Steps:

  1. Choose a Lead Applicant Organization
    • This entity will receive and manage the funds
  2. Develop a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU)
    • Required for all collaborating partners
  3. Complete and Submit the Application
    • Ensure all required fields are filled before submission

Saving Your Application

Applicants can save progress and return later using the platform’s “Save my progress” feature.

Accessibility & Inclusive Application Options

DIF is committed to accessibility. Alternative formats are available, including:

  • Zoom or phone submissions
  • Word or PDF formats
  • Captioning and ASL interpretation (for webinars)

Use of AI in Applications

Applicants may use AI tools for drafting responses. However, DIF emphasizes authenticity and real-world experience—applications should reflect your organization’s actual work, challenges, and impact.

What Happens Next?

  • Applications are reviewed through a participatory grantmaking process
  • Final decisions are made by a committee of disability advocates and funders
  • Applicants will be notified by November 20, 2026

Application Questions (Overview)

Applicants will respond to short narrative questions (max 250 words each), covering:

  • Collaboration goals and partner roles
  • Use of anti-ableist frameworks
  • Project plans and expected outcomes
  • Challenges and support needs
  • Monitoring and evaluation strategies

Required Documents

  • Project budget (estimated)
  • Combined organizational budget
  • 501(c)(3) documentation or fiscal sponsorship agreement
  • List of current funders
  • Geographic scope of work

The Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 presents a powerful opportunity for disability-led organizations to collaborate, scale impact, and drive systemic change. If your work centers inclusion, justice, and collective action, this grant could be a strong fit.

 Be sure to submit your application before May 20, 2026.

Visit Here, for Frequently Asked Questions.

The post Apply Now: Disability Inclusion Fund 2026 Grant for Disability Justice Projects (Up to $150,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of the Free State (UFS) 2027 Applications Now Open: Complete Step-by-Step Guide for Undergraduate, Postgraduate, and International Applicants
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The University of the Free State (UFS) has officially opened applications for the 2027 academic year, providing prospective students with an opportunity to access quality higher education in South Africa. Applications are free of charge and conducted entirely online through the UFS application portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early, particularly for highly competitive […]

The post University of the Free State (UFS) 2027 Applications Now Open: Complete Step-by-Step Guide for Undergraduate, Postgraduate, and International Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of the Free State (UFS) has officially opened applications for the 2027 academic year, providing prospective students with an opportunity to access quality higher education in South Africa.

Applications are free of charge and conducted entirely online through the UFS application portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early, particularly for highly competitive programmes with earlier closing dates.

This detailed guide provides accurate and complete information on the application process, deadlines, requirements, document submission, and official support contacts.

Application Opening Date

  • Applications opened on: Wednesday, 1 April 2026
  • Application fee: Free

Step-by-Step UFS Online Application Process

The UFS online application system consists of eight mandatory steps:

  1. Start Application
  2. Selection Information
  3. Study Choices
  4. Verification
  5. Personal Information
  6. Uploads
  7. Agreement
  8. Completion

All steps must be completed for the application to be successfully submitted.

After submission:

  • You will receive a confirmation email
  • You will be issued a student number
  • You can track your application via the Self-Service portal

Types of Applications Available

1. Undergraduate Applications

  • Open to Grade 12 learners and high school graduates
  • Applicants may select up to two study programmes
  • Must meet minimum admission requirements

2. Postgraduate Applications

  • Includes Honours, Postgraduate Diplomas, Master’s, and PhD programmes
  • Requires academic transcripts and supporting documents

3. Specialized Schools

  • Business School
  • School of Financial Planning Law

Applicants must consult the relevant faculty or prospectus for detailed programme requirements.

Click HERE to apply for the Scholarship.

Important Application Deadlines (2027 Intake)

Undergraduate Deadlines

31 May 2026

  • MB ChB (Medicine)
  • Occupational Therapy
  • Optometry
  • Physiotherapy
  • Radiation Science
  • Biokinetics
  • Dietetics
  • Sports Coaching

31 July 2026

  • Nursing
  • Social Work
  • Architecture

31 August 2026

  • All international undergraduate applications

30 September 2026

  • Transfer students from other institutions
  • All remaining undergraduate programmes (selection & non-selection)

Applicants should apply well before deadlines, especially for health-related programmes.

International Applicants: Requirements

International students must meet the following requirements:

  • Obtain a valid study visa for South Africa (endorsed for UFS)
  • Provide a Universities South Africa (USAf) Certificate of Exemption if they do not hold a South African National Senior Certificate
  • Submit certified academic documents
  • Submit predicted or mid-year results (for Cambridge or equivalent systems)

International postgraduate applicants must also provide:

  • SAQA (South African Qualifications Authority) certificate

Required Documents for Application

For Current Grade 12 Learners

  • Final Grade 11 results OR
  • June Grade 12 results

For Completed Grade 12 Applicants

  • Final Grade 12 certificate

For Postgraduate Applicants

  • Academic transcripts
  • Degree certificates
  • Supporting documents

Important Notes

  • Maximum file size: 5 MB per document
  • Documents should be correctly uploaded to avoid delays
  • Missing or incorrect documents will delay processing

Submitting Results and Documents

  • If applying with Grade 11 results, Grade 12 results must later be uploaded via the Self-Service portal
  • Ensure all documents are accurate and complete
  • Uploading incorrect information may delay your application

Monitoring Your Application Status

After submission:

  • You will receive an acknowledgement email with your student number
  • Updates will be sent via email
  • You can check your status on:
    https://myapplication.ufs.ac.za/

Important:

  • Application status will not be shared via phone calls
  • Applicants must monitor their email regularly

Official Support Contacts (Emails & Help Channels)

Undergraduate Application Support

  • Email: applications@ufs.ac.za
  • WhatsApp: KovsieChat – 087 240 6370
  • Phone: +27 (0)51 401 3000

Postgraduate Diploma & Honours Support

  • Email: studentadmin@ufs.ac.za
  • Phone: +27 (0)51 401 9111 (Option 1)

Master’s and Doctoral Applications

  • Email: UFSPostgradAdmin@ufs.ac.za

International Applicants (Documents & Results)

  • Email: oiastudentadmin@ufs.ac.za

Important Application Guidelines

  • Only apply for programmes where you meet all requirements
  • Ensure all uploaded documents are complete and accurate
  • Keep your contact details updated
  • Regularly check your email for updates
  • Apply early for competitive programmes

Data Privacy and Compliance

The University of the Free State processes personal data in accordance with the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA). Applicants’ data is securely stored and used for academic and administrative purposes.

Applicants are responsible for ensuring that all submitted information is accurate and up to date.

Click HERE to learn more about the University of the Free State (UFS) 2027 Applications.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY.

The post University of the Free State (UFS) 2027 Applications Now Open: Complete Step-by-Step Guide for Undergraduate, Postgraduate, and International Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers from the Global South
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Danish Institute for Human Rights has launched its Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026, a fully funded initiative designed to strengthen international research collaboration with academics primarily based in the Global South and other underrepresented regions. This prestigious programme offers selected researchers the opportunity to work with one of Europe’s leading human rights institutions while […]

The post Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Danish Institute for Human Rights has launched its Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026, a fully funded initiative designed to strengthen international research collaboration with academics primarily based in the Global South and other underrepresented regions. This prestigious programme offers selected researchers the opportunity to work with one of Europe’s leading human rights institutions while contributing to high-impact global research.

If you are a scholar passionate about human rights, governance, justice systems, or digital transformation, this is an exceptional opportunity to gain international exposure, expand your academic network, and produce meaningful research outputs.

What is the Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme?

The Danish Institute for Human Rights is Denmark’s National Human Rights Institution and equality body. It works nationally and globally to promote and protect human rights through research, policy advice, and strategic partnerships.

Through this new visiting researcher programme, the Institute seeks to develop new and strengthen existing collaborations with researchers from underrepresented regions. The programme emphasizes equitable partnerships, mutual learning, and knowledge exchange.

Key Research Themes for 2026

Successful applicants will work within one of three major thematic areas:

1. Technology and Human Rights

This theme focuses on how emerging and existing technologies impact human rights and democratic governance in the Global South.

Possible research topics include:

  • Digital rights and freedoms
  • AI governance and accountability
  • Human rights impact assessments of technology
  • Inclusive digital regulations and policy frameworks
  • Rights-respecting digital transitions through development cooperation

2. National Human Rights Institutions and Preventing Violent Extremism

This area examines how National Human Rights Institutions (NHRIs) can contribute to peace, security, and the prevention of violent extremism in Sahelian and coastal West African contexts.

Possible research topics include:

  • Security governance and human rights
  • Radicalization prevention strategies
  • Human rights-based approaches to peacebuilding
  • Policy recommendations for regional frameworks
  • Population protection strategies

3. Customary Justice Systems and Human Rights Standards

This research theme explores how customary law, informal justice systems, community paralegals, and formal justice institutions can better protect marginalized communities.

Priority groups include:

  • Women
  • Children
  • People with disabilities

Applicants in this area should draw on case studies from at least two of the following countries:

  • Ethiopia
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • Uganda
  • Zambia

Programme Duration

  • Affiliation with the Institute for up to 6 months
  • Minimum 2-week visit to Copenhagen required
  • Hybrid participation models are welcome

Benefits of the Programme

Selected researchers will receive substantial support, including:

  • Research stipend / honorarium
  • Travel costs to and from Copenhagen
  • Visa support and costs
  • Accommodation in Copenhagen
  • Academic mentorship and institutional support
  • Access to policy and international research networks
  • Opportunity to publish policy briefs, working papers, or academic articles

Required Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Current CV (maximum 3 pages)
  • One relevant publication in English or French
  • Project proposal (1–5 pages) including aims, methodology, outputs, and relevance

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Human rights expertise and experience
  • Affiliation with an academic institution in the Global South
  • Quality of previous publications
  • Feasibility of project proposal
  • Relevance to the thematic focus areas

Why You Should Apply

This programme is more than a research visit. It is a platform for global collaboration, policy influence, and academic growth. Participants will engage with leading experts, contribute to real-world human rights solutions, and build long-term partnerships for future funding opportunities.

Application Deadline

1 June 2026

How to Apply

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more internship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

The Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026 is one of the best fully funded research opportunities for scholars from the Global South. If your work intersects with justice, technology, governance, or human rights, this programme could be a major step forward in your academic career.

Apply early and prepare a strong proposal that clearly demonstrates relevance, impact, and feasibility.

The post Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany is now open for applications. This prestigious fully funded fellowship is designed for researchers, policy experts, think tank professionals, and practitioners working on Turkey-related issues. Hosted by the Centre for Applied Turkey Studies (CATS) at the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP), the fellowship offers a […]

The post CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany is now open for applications. This prestigious fully funded fellowship is designed for researchers, policy experts, think tank professionals, and practitioners working on Turkey-related issues. Hosted by the Centre for Applied Turkey Studies (CATS) at the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP), the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to conduct impactful research in one of Europe’s leading academic and policy environments.

Applicants from all countries can apply, and there is no application fee.

The fellowship runs for 6 to 12 months, beginning from September 2026, and selected fellows will be based in Berlin, Germany.

Why Apply for the CATS Fellowship?

This program is ideal for scholars and professionals seeking to deepen their expertise in Turkish politics, security, climate policy, digital transformation, and geopolitical affairs while collaborating with leading researchers in Germany.

Selected fellows will gain access to a world-class research network, publication opportunities, and financial support to focus fully on their projects.

Fellowship Benefits

The CATS Fellowship is fully funded and includes:

  • Monthly grant of €3,000
  • Travel allowance to and from Berlin
  • Budget for research trips
  • Tuition support
  • Participation in field trips
  • Additional family support for fellows accompanied by children
  • Collaboration opportunities with CATS and SWP researchers
  • Possibility of publishing research in peer-reviewed CATS and SWP outlets

Research Topics for 2026/2027

Applicants are invited to submit proposals under the following themes:

1. Digitalisation and Artificial Intelligence

Research may explore Turkey’s AI strategy, digital governance, cyber resilience, defense modernization, labor markets, and economic transformation through emerging technologies.

2. Critical Raw Materials (CRMs)

Topics include Turkey’s rare earth minerals strategy, extraction policies, geopolitical competition, industrial upgrading, and EU-Turkey cooperation on critical resources.

3. Energy Transition and Climate Change

Applicants may focus on Turkey’s climate commitments, water scarcity, renewable energy strategy, governance reforms, and environmental security.

4. Turkish Foreign and Security Policy Amid Geopolitical Turmoil

Areas include NATO dynamics, Black Sea security, South Caucasus relations, regional power competition, maritime strategy, and energy corridors.

Who Should Apply?

This fellowship is suitable for:

  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Academics and scholars
  • Think tank analysts
  • Policy experts
  • Practitioners in relevant sectors
  • Researchers with strong publication records
  • PhD candidates with proven expertise in Turkey-related research

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit one single PDF file containing:

  • Cover letter
  • Research proposal (maximum 5 pages)
  • Research objectives and relevance
  • State of research overview
  • Methodology
  • Bibliography
  • CV with list of publications
  • Academic transcripts and/or diplomas

Applications may be submitted in English or German.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Academic and professional merit
  • Relevance to fellowship themes
  • Feasibility and coherence of proposal
  • Public outreach value of the project

Preference may be given to applicants who have not previously received CATS funding in cases of equal merit.

Deadline

The application deadline is 30 April 2026. Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

Send your complete application as a single PDF document via email to:

CATSapplication@swp-berlin.org

For inquiries: CATS@swp-berlin.org

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

If you are a researcher or policy professional looking for a fully funded fellowship in Europe, the CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Germany is an outstanding opportunity. With generous funding, access to influential networks, and a chance to contribute to high-level policy research, this fellowship can significantly advance your academic and professional career.

The post CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
